prompt: Assess the likely impact of changing mux trailing-slash redirect behavior. Identify implementation areas and regression surfaces before editing.

expected_symbols.name: Router.StrictSlash
expected_symbols.why: Configures trailing-slash behavior for new routes.

expected_symbols.name: Route.Match
expected_symbols.why: Evaluates route matchers and records match state.

expected_symbols.name: Route.addRegexpMatcher
expected_symbols.why: Passes strict-slash options into path regexp construction.

expected_symbols.name: newRouteRegexp
expected_symbols.why: Compiles route templates and redirect-aware path matching expressions.

expected_claims.text: Trailing-slash behavior is configured through Router.StrictSlash.
Route matcher construction is an impact surface because it carries strict-slash options into regexp compilation.
Route.Match and router request handling should be checked because redirect behavior is observed during matching.
Regression coverage should include mux request matching tests and route regexp tests.

forbidden_claims.text: Strict slash behavior is isolated from route regexp construction.
Path prefix routes and subrouters cannot be affected by trailing-slash configuration.
